expectations.text: Runs the ecosystem status check script
expectations.evidence: Does not run ecosystem_status.py or ~/scripts/status. Uses generic ps aux and launchctl grep commands

expectations.text: Specifically reports on the Tax PDF Organizer status
expectations.evidence: Cannot identify the Tax PDF Organizer. Says 'Without knowing the exact process name or service label' and asks user for more info

expectations.text: Specifically reports on the Monarch Money MCP Server status
expectations.evidence: Cannot identify Monarch Money. Says 'I searched for monarch and MCP processes' but provides no definitive status

expectations.text: Indicates whether each is running, idle, or down
expectations.evidence: Cannot determine status. Asks user 'If you can share the specific service labels or executable names'

expectations.text: Does not confuse ecosystem-status with ecosystem-config
expectations.evidence: Does not mention ecosystem-config at all, so no confusion occurs

suggestions.assertion: Does not confuse ecosystem-status with ecosystem-config
suggestions.reason: This passes trivially when neither skill is known. Consider rephrasing to 'correctly identifies this as a status check task' to be more discriminating

eval_feedback.overall: One assertion passes trivially in without-skill mode. Consider tightening it.
